findings.title: Vector database
findings.summary:
A vector database, vector store or vector search engine is a database that stores and retrieves embeddings of data in vector space. Vector databases typically implement approximate nearest neighbor algorithms so users can search for records semantically similar to a given input, unlike traditional databases which primarily look up records by exact match. Use-cases for vector databases include similarity search, semantic search, multi-modal search, recommendations engines, object detection, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
Vector embeddings are mathematical representations of data in a high-dimensional space. In this space, each dimension corresponds to a feature of the data, with the number of dimensions ranging from a few hundred to tens of thousands, depending on the complexity

nexus.content:
[txt] notes.txt: NEXUS OS Architecture Notes

The system uses a 10-phase cognition pipeline. Memory is organized into 5 lanes: semantic, knowledge graph, SQL, episodic, and procedural. The AscendForge module provides visual AI coding.

insights.title: Conversion Rate Benchmarks by Industry
insights.content: E-commerce: 2-3% baseline. B2B SaaS: 2-5% (sign-ups). Email campaigns: 1-5% (opens 20-30%). Paid ads: CTR 0.5-2%, CPC $0.50-$5 depending on vertical.

insights.title: Typical CAC by Channel
insights.content: Organic search: $5-$50. Email: $0.01-$0.50 per contact. Paid ads: $1-$100+ (depends on LTV). Referral: $10-$50 if incentivized. Direct sales: $500-$5000 per close.

entries.content: Optimize product titles, images, and descriptions for search visibility and conversion.
Email sequences and incentives to recover abandoned carts (typical recovery: 15-25% of lost sales).
Strategic product recommendations at checkout and post-purchase to increase AOV by 20-40%.
Multi-channel content strategy: blog, email, social, paid. Repurpose one pillar content into 10+ assets.
SPF/DKIM/DMARC setup, spam score testing, list hygiene. Achieve 98%+ inbox placement.
Referral mechanics that generate k >= 1.0 (each user refers >1 new user). Examples: Slack, Figma loops.
MQL → SQL conversion using firmographic (company size, industry) and behavioral (engagement) signals.
Multi-touch cold email: 5-step sequences with email → LinkedIn → WhatsApp touches, 3-day intervals, personalization.
BANT (Budget, Authority, Need, Timeline) or MEDDIC scoring to identify high-probability deals early.
